assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '## Classification: Code Change Request'. report.md table: '| 30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs:60 | Code change request | Sub-task created |'. subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion -- the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: Suggestion'. Reasoning section explicitly cites: '"should also" -- While "should" can sometimes be directive, the addition of "also" softens it to a suggestion', '"would help" -- This is clearly suggestive language', '"Something like:" -- The phrase "something like" explicitly frames the SQL example as one possible approach'. All three suggestive language indicators are identified and explained.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: Nit'. States 'No sub-task is created.' report.md table: '| 30003 | ... | Nit | No sub-task |'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: Question'. States 'No sub-task is created for questions.' report.md table: '| 30004 | ... | Question | No sub-task |'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 3), '## Files to Modify' (line 7), '## Implementation Notes' (line 10), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 16). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), and it satisfies all template requirements.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 21-22: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. The exact URL matches.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md lines 24-30: '## Review Context' section includes 'Comment ID: 30001', 'Author: reviewer-a', file path, and the full original comment text: 'The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files -- it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only 6 files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are markdown classification/report/sub-task description files. No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report itself is a verification report, not a code modification.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md table lists all four comments with classifications. The report's Review Feedback section explicitly states 'Four review comments were classified' and provides detailed analysis for each.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merging, or merge commands. The overall status is 'WARN' with the note 'One code change request from review feedback requires attention.' The report is purely a verification report with findings and sub-task creation, not a merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE -- only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is new) |'. The detailed section (lines 123-125) confirms: 'Only new test files are present in the PR. The file tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present on the base branch). No existing test files were modified or deleted. All 5 test functions are additions. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '### Convention Upgrade Check' section that explicitly evaluates two sources: (1) 'CONVENTIONS.md: No CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the fixture data' and (2) 'Codebase patterns: In this eval context, there is no real codebase to inspect for patterns'. It concludes: 'upgrades must be backed by a concrete CONVENTIONS.md section or a counted codebase pattern. General knowledge that "indexes are a database best practice" is explicitly insufficient for an upgrade.' report.md also states for 30002: 'No CONVENTIONS.md exists in the target repository fixture and no codebase patterns could be verified, so the suggestion was not upgraded to a code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task -- the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. review-30002.md states: 'No sub-task is created for suggestions.' report.md table: '| 30002 | ... | Suggestion | No sub-task (suggestive language, no convention backing) |'. The Convention Upgrade Check in review-30002.md confirms no convention evidence supports an upgrade.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR -- the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 121: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on this PR. The 3-criteria detection (author is github-actions[bot], body contains ## Eval Results, body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer) found no matches.' All three detection criteria are explicitly named. Test Quality row shows 'PASS' (line 13), confirming Eval Quality N/A did not negatively affect the combination.
